title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Independent Network Trial
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an evidence-backed incident resolution packet with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed lab case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Increasing AP transmit power fixes weak coverage without affecting clients or roaming.

stimulus.title: Net Wireless Performance Faults failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Increasing AP transmit power fixes weak coverage without affecting clients or roaming.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Wireless Performance Faults condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Performance Faults applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.
